text.title: ops-financial-planning Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ops-financial-planning. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Runway (months) = Cash on hand / Monthly burn rate. Minimum safe runway by stage: pre-revenue = 12+ months, early revenue = 6+ months, growing = 3+ months, profitable = 3+ months (emergency fund).
rule.rationale: Runway is the primary financial safety metric. Below 3 months, a single bad month becomes an existential threat. The emergency fund (3x monthly expenses, separate account) is the profitable-stage equivalent.

rule.statement: Lock a minimum 20% reserve before any discretionary spend. Adjust only after the emergency fund is fully funded (3x monthly expenses).
rule.rationale: The reserve percentage is the only number that should never flex. It protects against cashflow surprises before addressing growth or owner compensation.
